free_text: Ratio of cross sections for the production of the
44Sc and 44mSc isomer pair in heavy ion reactions.
English translation
U-300 cyclotron of the Laboratory for
Nuclear Reactions
The ion current was 0.1 - 2 mu-A in the various
experiments, the bombardment time ranged from 15
minutes to 2 hours.
Scintillation spectrometer and a 100-channel
AI-100 pulse-height analyzer were used.
No source of uncertainty for the error
bars is given.
*By compiler* Decay-data were taken by authors from
B.S.Dzhelepov, L.K.Peker, Decay Schemes of
Radioactive Nuclei, AN SSSR, 1958, p. 91.
No explanation of relative units is given.
Stacks of 6-12 aluminum foils from 3 to 9 mu-m thick
were irradiated.
Figure 3a
No explanation of relative units is given.

were irradiated.

No explanation of relative units is given.
Stacks of aluminum foil 6 mu-m thick were used, each
foil being coated with 0.6-0.7 mg/cm2 of sulfur.
